response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates exceptional software engineering breadth and 15+ years of experience, this is a fundamental domain mismatch. The role requires native Japanese proficiency (completely absent from resume), deep C expertise for embedded firmware (minimal, 2014 only), and specialized knowledge in security protocols, networking, and kernel-level systems. The candidate's career focuses on web, mobile, and full-stack applications—not embedded firmware or security. The critical language barrier alone is disqualifying against an explicit required qualification.
strengths: Extensive software development background (15+ years) with proven ability to learn diverse tech stacks and domains
Strong testing culture and code quality focus evidenced by TDD practices, open-source contributions, and infrastructure work
Recent full-stack engineering role at re:cinq (2025-present) with exposure to infrastructure and complex system design
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency mentioned—explicitly listed as 'native-level' required qualification, completely absent
Minimal C programming experience (brief 2014-2015 role only); primary languages are JavaScript, DLang, TypeScript—misaligned with role's 'strong C proficiency' requirement
No demonstrated expertise in security technologies (encryption, authentication, tamper detection), networking protocols (SNMP, NETCONF, layer-2/3), or embedded firmware—core to the role

key_factors.factor: Domain mismatch: web/full-stack focus vs. embedded firmware/security specialization
Weak C and kernel-level systems experience relative to role requirements
